aiTitle: Make issue popup scrollable on small screens

addedBlocks:
## pencil
The Pencil MCP server is the editor for reading and writing to .pen files and gives you tools to be an expert design generator and validator specifically for web and mobile applications and websites.

- IMPORTANT: The contents of the .pen files are encrypted and can be only access via the "pencil" MCP tools. DO NOT use "Read" or "Grep" tools to read contents of .pen files.
- IMPORTANT: Make sure to ONLY use the "pencil" MCP tools when reading or searching contents of .pen files (batch_get) and making changes inside .pen files (batch_design).
- IMPORTANT: Always follow the tool definitions and formats for the "pencil" MCP tools.

Available tools:
- "get_editor_state({ include_schema })": Start with this tool if you are unaware of the current editor state. Use this tool to determine in the beginning of a task, to understand what is the currently active .pen file you want to work on and the current user selection in it and other essential context information.
- "open_document(filePathOrNew)": When there is no active editor opened, pass 'new' to create an empty .pen file or if the user explicitly ask to open a specific .pen file use its path to open it.
- "get_guidelines(category?, name?, params?)": Load guides and styles for working with .pen files.
- "batch_get(patterns, nodeIds)": Retrieve nodes by searching for matching patterns or by reading node ids in batches. Use this for discovering and understand .pen files.
- "batch_design(operations)": Use it when designing with .pen files to execute multiple insert/copy/update/replace/move/delete/image operations in a single call. IMPORTANT: MAKE SURE TO FOLLOW THE OPERATION SYNTAX DEFINED IN THE TOOL'S DESCRIPTION. Aim for maximum 25 operations per call—enough to make meaningful progress without overwhelming the system. Available operations (used as a small script where every line a single operation call):
	- Insert: foo=I("parent", { ... })
	- Copy: baz=C("nodeid", "parent", { ...})
	- Replace: foo2=R("nodeid1/nodeid2", {...})
	- Update: U(foo+"/nodeid", {...})
	- Dele… [truncated]

content.thinking:
The user is reporting a bug: the "新建问题" (Create New Issue) popup/modal is not scrollable when the window height is insufficient, preventing users from accessing all form fields.

Let me find the relevant frontend component.

content.text: Now let me check the UModal content area to add overflow scrolling.

content.content: 50 51 52 53 108 109

content.text: The fix is to make the modal content scrollable when it exceeds the viewport height. I need to add `max-height` and `overflow-y: auto` to the `.modal-form` container.

content.content: The file /Users/ck/Git/matrix/devtrack/frontend/app/pages/app/issues/index.vue has been updated successfully.

content.text: Added `max-height: 90vh` and `overflow-y: auto` to `.modal-form` so the popup becomes scrollable when the viewport is too short to show all fields.
<ide_opened_file>The user opened the file /Users/ck/Git/matrix/devtrack/frontend/app/pages/app/issues/index.vue in the IDE. This may or may not be related to the current task.</ide_opened_file>
commit all changes and push to env/test

diff --git a/backend/tests/test_opencode_runner.py b/backend/tests/test_opencode_runner.py
index a337f39..4f88e9a 100644
--- a/backend/tests/test_opencode_runner.py
+++ b/backend/tests/test_opencode_runner.py
@@ -16,24 +16,21 @@ class TestOpenCodeRunner:
         self.prompt = PromptFactory(llm_model="deepseek-chat")
         self.runner = OpenCodeRunner(self.config)
 
-    def test_generate_config(self):
-        config = self.runner.generate_config("deepseek-chat")
-        assert "provider" in config
-        provider = list(config["provider"].values())[0]
-        assert provider["options"]["apiKey"] == "sk-test123"
-        assert provider["options"]["baseURL"] == "https://api.deepseek.com/v1"
-        assert "model" in config
+    def test_get_model_id(self):
+        model_id = self.runner._get_model_id("deepseek-chat")
+        assert "deepseek-chat" in model_id
 
-    def test_generate_config_model_in_provider(self):
-        config = self.runner.generate_config("deepseek-chat")
-        provider = list(config["provider"].values())[0]
-        assert "deepseek-chat" in provider["models"]
+    def test_get_env_sets_api_key(self):
+        self.config.name = "deepseek"
+        self.config.save()
+        runner = OpenCodeRunner(self.config)
+        env = runner._get_env()
+        assert "DEEPSEEK_API_KEY" in env
+        assert env["DEEPSEEK_API_KEY"] == "sk-test123"
 
-    @patch("apps.ai.opencode.os.path.exists", return_value=True)
-    @patch("apps.ai.opencode.os.unlink")
-    @patch("builtins.open", new_callable=mock_open)
+    @patch("apps.ai.opencode._opencode_bin", return_value="/usr/local/bin/opencode")
     @patch("apps.ai.opencode.subprocess.run")
-    def test_run_success(self, mock_run, mock_file, mock_unlink, mock_exists):
+    def test_run_success(self, mock_run, mock_bin):
         mock_run.return_value = MagicMock(
             returncode=0,
             stdout='{"target_field": "cause", "content": "分析结果"}',
@@ -47,17 +44,16 @@ class TestOpenCodeRunner:
         assert "分析结果" in result
         mock_run.assert_called_once()
 
-    @patch("apps.ai.opencode.os.path.exists", return_value=False)
-    @patch("builtins.open", new_callable=mock_open)
+    @patch("apps.ai.opencode._opencode_bin", return_value="/usr/local/bin/opencode")
     @patch("apps.ai.opencode.subprocess.run")
-    def test_run_cleans_up_config(self, mock_run, mock_file, mock_exists):
+    def test_run_invokes_subprocess(self, mock_run, mock_bin):
         mock_run.return_value = MagicMock(returncode=0, stdout="ok", stderr="")
         self.runner.run(repo_path="/tmp/test_repo", prompt="test", model="m")
-        # Config file cleanup attempted (os.path.exists returned False so unlink not called)
+        mock_run.assert_called_once()
 
-    @patch("builtins.open", new_callable=mock_open)
+    @patch("apps.ai.opencode._opencode_bin", return_value="/usr/local/bin/opencode")
     @patch("apps.ai.opencode.subprocess.run")
-    def test_run_timeout(self, mock_run, mock_file):
+    def test_run_timeout(self, mock_run, mock_bin):
         mock_run.side_effect = subprocess.TimeoutExpired(cmd="opencode", timeout=120)
         with pytest.raises(subprocess.TimeoutExpired):
             self.runner.run(repo_path="/tmp/test_repo", prompt="test", model="m")

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(ui): make create issue modal scrollable + update opencode tests and version

- Add max-height and overflow-y to issue creation modal for small viewports
- Refactor opencode runner tests to match updated implementation
- Bump version to 1.0.0
- Add soft delete plan doc

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
